spec_match.rationale: Frames the spec change exactly as the chromestatus entry and Open UI explainer do — 'this attribute replaces the roving-tabindex pattern' — with the canonical radio-group example used in ARIA Authoring Practices.

interactive.rationale: Side-by-side live radio groups both keyboard-navigable with arrows; the old version really uses tabindex juggling + keydown JS, the new version really uses the focusgroup attribute on the wrapper. Visitor can keyboard-test both and compare the line counts.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises a side-by-side comparison with line count; the page delivers two working radio groups with the JS implementation visible and the focusgroup version using one attribute.

edge_cases.rationale: Happy paths for arrow nav and Home/End are covered, but no demo of: focus loss when the active element is removed (the new attribute handles this; the old code may not), Tab exit semantics, and ARIA orientation auto-sync (the old code has to be told 'vertical', the new attribute can be inferred).

design_system.rationale: Variables-driven, no static-card, comparison columns use bg-paper with borders — consistent with the design system.

openQuestions.title: Surface the actual line-count delta
openQuestions.detail: Blurb says 'count the lines saved'. Render a small footer label like 'Old: 47 lines. New: 1 attribute.' so the comparison is quantified rather than left to the visitor to scroll through the code blocks.

openQuestions.title: Show the focus-loss recovery edge case
openQuestions.detail: If the active focused item is removed (DOM mutation), the roving-tabindex implementation typically drops focus to the body. The native focusgroup spec is supposed to handle this by promoting the next sibling. Add an 'add/remove option' button to both columns and demonstrate the difference.

openQuestions.title: Add orientation auto-inference
openQuestions.detail: The old code hard-codes the vertical axis; the new attribute supports `block inline` and adapts to writing-mode. Add a `writing-mode: vertical-rl` toggle so the comparison includes the i18n win, not just line count.

summary: Excellent before/after that sells the spec change with a fair direct comparison. Quantify the line saving in-page, add a focus-loss-recovery edge case, and demo the writing-mode adaptation to make the win complete.
